fields.description:
ABOUT THE TEAM:

OpenAI’s mission is to ensure that artificial general intelligence benefits all of humanity. Achieving this mission requires building a team of exceptional people from around the world. The Head of Global Mobility and Immigration will play a critical role in enabling OpenAI to attract, hire, and retain global talent by designing and leading immigration and mobility programs that support a rapidly growing and internationally distributed workforce.

OpenAI competes globally for some of the world’s most sought-after technical and research talent. This role requires a creative and solutions-oriented leader who can navigate complex immigration systems and develop innovative pathways to bring exceptional international talent to OpenAI. The ideal candidate is comfortable thinking beyond traditional playbooks and designing thoughtful, compliant strategies that enable the company to hire and retain the people needed to advance our mission while supporting our global expansion.


ABOUT THE ROLE:

The Head of Global Mobility and Immigration will lead and scale OpenAI’s immigration, relocation, and mobility tax programs during a period of rapid company growth and global expansion. This role requires someone who can operate strategically while also building and refining the operational systems that enable the company to hire and support talent from around the world.

You will partner closely with Legal, Recruiting, People Operations, Finance, and leadership teams to ensure OpenAI’s immigration and mobility programs support the company’s growth while managing legal and compliance risk. You will also lead and develop a high-performing team responsible for delivering exceptional support to employees and candidates navigating immigration and relocation processes.

This role requires someone who thrives in fast-moving environments, is comfortable operating without rigid playbooks, and can design creative solutions tailored to OpenAI’s unique pace, scale, and global hiring needs.


WHY THIS ROLE IS UNIQUE

OpenAI competes globally for some of the most sought-after technical and research talent in the world. Many of the individuals we hire have highly specialized expertise and international backgrounds that require thoughtful and creative immigration solutions.

The Head of Global Mobility and Immigration will play a critical role in enabling the company to hire exceptional talent quickly and compliantly across jurisdictions. This role will involve navigating complex immigration scenarios, supporting extraordinary talent pathways, and designing scalable mobility programs that allow OpenAI to build a truly global workforce.

Success in this role requires creativity, sound judgment, and the ability to develop compliant solutions that enable the company to move quickly in a highly competitive global talent market.


IN THIS ROLE YOU WILL:

 - Develop and lead OpenAI’s global immigration and mobility strategy to support the company’s hiring and workforce planning goals.

 - Design scalable programs, standard operating procedures (SOPs) and policies that enable global hiring while managing compliance, risk, and operational complexity.

 - Partner with Recruiting, M&A and leadership to develop creative, compliant strategies that enable OpenAI to recruit and retain the world’s top international talent and advise on new market expansion from an immigration perspective.

 - Own OpenAI’s global immigration program, including visa sponsorship, work authorization, oversight and audit of employer verification processes, permanent residence processes, and compliance obligations across jurisdictions.

 - Design and manage global mobility programs, including relocations, international assignments, and cross-border transfers.

 - Provide strategic guidance to leadership on complex immigration, relocation, mobility tax and cross-border employment issues.

 - Partner with Legal, Finance, and Tax teams to manage mobility-related regulatory and tax considerations.

 - Build and lead a high-performing immigration and mobility team and develop the processes and systems needed to scale the function.

 - Manage relationships with external immigration counsel and vendors to ensure high-quality case management and employee support.

 - Establish metrics, reporting, and governance structures to track immigration activity, program effectiveness, and risk areas.

 - Continuously improve processes, tools, and employee experience as the company scales its global workforce.


YOU MIGHT THRIVE IN THIS ROLE IF YOU:

 - 12+ years of experience leading immigration and/or global mobility programs in a fast-growing company or multinational organization.

 - Significant experience leading immigration and/or global mobility programs in a fast-growing company or multinational organization.

 - Demonstrated experience building and scaling complex global mobility and immigration programs at a fast-paced, globally distributed company with a significant international workforce.

 - Deep expertise in U.S. immigration processes (e.g., H-1B, O-1, PERM, green card sponsorship) and familiarity with global immigration frameworks.

 - Experience designing or scaling global mobility programs, including international relocations and cross-border assignments.

 - Demonstrated experience leading and developing high-performing teams.

 - Strong judgment and ability to navigate complex immigration and compliance issues.

 - Ability to translate legal and regulatory requirements into scalable operational programs.

 - Experience managing external immigration counsel and mobility vendors to support top talent through work transitions.

 - Exceptional cross-functional collaboration skills, the ability to influence stakeholders and inspire trust and confidence across all levels and within the organization.

 - Thrives in fast-moving environments where priorities evolve quickly.

 - Creative and resourceful in solving complex immigration and mobility challenges.

 - Comfortable operating without rigid playbooks and designing new approaches where existing models don’t fit.

 - Able to develop innovative, compliant solutions that enable the company to compete globally for rare and exceptional talent.

 - Highly operational, with the ability to design systems and processes that scale with rapid company growth.

 - Mission-driven and motivated by helping build a global team working on transformative technology.

About OpenAI

OpenAI is an AI research and deployment company dedicated to ensuring that general-purpose artificial intelligence benefits all of humanity. We push the boundaries of the capabilities of AI systems and seek to safely deploy them to the world through our products. AI is an extremely powerful tool that must be created with safety and human needs at its core, and to achieve our mission, we must encompass and value the many different perspectives, voices, and experiences that form the full spectrum of humanity. 

We are an equal opportunity employer, and we do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, age, veteran status, disability, genetic information, or other applicable legally protected characteristic.

For additional information, please see OpenAI’s Affirmative Action and Equal Employment Opportunity Policy Statement https://cdn.openai.com/policies/eeo-policy-statement.pdf.

Background checks for applicants will be administered in accordance with applicable law, and qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records will be considered for employment consistent with those laws, including the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers, and the California Fair Chance Act, for US-based candidates. For unincorporated Los Angeles County workers: we reasonably believe that criminal history may have a direct, adverse and negative relationship with the following job duties, potentially resulting in the withdrawal of a conditional offer of employment: protect computer hardware entrusted to you from theft, loss or damage; return all computer hardware in your possession (including the data contained therein) upon termination of employment or end of assignment; and maintain the confidentiality of proprietary, confidential, and non-public information. In addition, job duties require access to secure and protected information technology systems and related data security obligations.

To notify OpenAI that you believe this job posting is non-compliant, please submit a report through this form https://form.asana.com/?d=57018692298241&k=5MqR40fZd7jlxVUh5J-UeA. No response will be provided to inquiries unrelated to job posting compliance.

We are committed to providing reasonable accommodations to applicants with disabilities, and requests can be made via this link https://form.asana.com/?k=bQ7w9h3iexRlicUdWRiwvg&d=57018692298241.

OpenAI Global Applicant Privacy Policy https://cdn.openai.com/policies/global-employee-and-contractor-privacy-policy.pdf

At OpenAI, we believe artificial intelligence has the potential to help people solve immense global challenges, and we want the upside of AI to be widely shared. Join us in shaping the future of technology.
fields.title: Head of Global Mobility and Immigration
